task_completion.description: Create an external tool builder named 'VersionGenerator'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Eclipse IDE, look for the 'Properties for AutoVer' dialog. In the 'Builders' section, there should be an entry named 'VersionGenerator' in the list of builders.

task_completion.description: Configure the builder Location and Working Directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Edit Configuration' dialog for the VersionGenerator builder. In the 'Main' tab, the 'Location' field should point to the script (e.g., using workspace variables like ${workspace_loc:/AutoVer/scripts/gen_version.sh}) and the 'Working Directory' should point to the project root (e.g., ${workspace_loc:/AutoVer}).

task_completion.description: Configure the builder to refresh resources upon completion
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the builder configuration dialog, under the 'Refresh' tab, the checkbox 'Refresh resources upon completion' must be checked.

task_completion.description: Configure the builder to run during Auto Build and Clean operations
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the builder configuration dialog, under the 'Build Options' tab, the checkboxes for 'During auto builds' and 'During a Clean' must be checked.

task_completion.description: Save the launch configuration as a shared file in the project root
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 'Common' tab in the builder configuration with 'Shared file' selected and the path set to '/AutoVer', OR look in the Eclipse Project Explorer to see 'VersionGenerator.launch' listed directly under the AutoVer project root.

task_completion.description: Ensure the build process successfully generates version.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Eclipse Project Explorer. Inside the 'AutoVer' project, under 'src/main/resources', there should be a file named 'version.txt'. Alternatively, look for the contents of 'version.txt' open in the Eclipse editor or script execution output in the Eclipse Console.

integrity.description: Agent used the Eclipse IDE GUI to configure the builder
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent used the Eclipse IDE GUI (Properties dialogs, External Tools Configurations) to create the builder, rather than just writing a raw XML .launch file in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually create the output files via terminal
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent did not manually create 'version.txt' or 'VersionGenerator.launch' using terminal commands (like 'touch', 'echo', or 'nano'). The files should be generated by Eclipse.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Eclipse IDE
integrity.visual_evidence: Verify that the agent is interacting with the actual Eclipse IDE workspace, not a different editor like VS Code or IntelliJ.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The project is named 'AutoVer' located at '/home/ga/eclipse-workspace/AutoVer'. The script to be executed is 'scripts/gen_version.sh'. The external builder must be named 'VersionGenerator' and its configuration saved as a shared file named 'VersionGenerator.launch' in the project root. The successful execution of the build process will generate 'src/main/resources/version.txt'.
